off", "write a handoff", "summarize where we are", or "save context". Produces

anchor: 6. Keep it scannable; omit conversational back-and-forth and anything re-derivable from the repo.
text:
7. Write the identical document to `.ai/handoff.md` (create `.ai/` if missing; overwrite the
   prior handoff — history lives in git). Stamp line 1:
   `_Saved: <date> · branch: <branch> · HEAD: <short-sha>_` so resume can drift-check.

old: `## Open threads` (next · blockers · dead ends) → `## Orient` (key files, commands, branch).
new:
`## Open threads` (next · blockers · dead ends) → `## Orient` (key files, commands, branch).
Write the same document to `.ai/handoff.md` with the `Saved` stamp as line 1; show it in chat as well.

anchor: - Keep it to roughly one screen; if it's longer, you're including chatter.
text: - Overwrite `.ai/handoff.md`; never accumulate multiple handoffs — one current handoff per project.

anchor: - **Code paste.** Inlining large snippets. Rule: link files/commands; keep it a map.
text: - **Chat-only handoff.** Emitted to chat but never persisted; the next session starts cold. Rule: always write `.ai/handoff.md`.
